headline: 467 airframes grounded across 25 states with $50M–$2B national exposure as gray zone enforcement accelerates and Raspberry Pi diversion confirms 40,000+ units in adversary weapon systems.

lead_story.title: NASAO Survey Documents 467 Grounded Airframes Across 23 States
lead_story.body: Oregon's NASAO Revision 2 white paper (February 2026) surveyed 25 state DOTs and confirmed 467 drone airframes are currently grounded or restricted across 23 states, creating national replacement exposure estimated between $50M and $2B. This represents the first authoritative count of enforcement impact following FCC Covered List expansion in December 2025. The grounding affects state transportation, emergency management, and public safety operations that relied on non-compliant platforms. With FCC retroactive revocation authority now active and multiple firmware certification deadlines converging in January 2027, this number is a floor, not a ceiling.
lead_story.action: Conduct a full inventory audit of your current fleet by April 19 to identify airframes that rely on FCC-listed entities (Anzu, SkyRover, Autel, Cogito/SPECTA). Flag units without firmware certification pathways and initiate replacement requisitions before Q3 budget freezes.

gray_zone.status: Risk score 0.850 (CRITICAL) — FCC Covered List, active retail inventory, direct DJI cloud connectivity confirmed.
gray_zone.development: Teardown analysis by Jon Sawyer confirmed SkyRover app connects directly to DJI cloud infrastructure using identical encryption keys. OTA update pipeline touches adversary infrastructure. Shell company structure traces to SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd. Despite FCC prohibition on new authorizations, S1 ($359) and X1 ($599) models remain available at retail with no recall mechanism in place.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Any agency that deployed S1/X1 units has active data exfiltration risk through DJI-controlled firmware update channels. Estimated 12–18 month deployed base still operational in field operations.
gray_zone.action: Ground all SkyRover units immediately. Quarantine devices from network connectivity. Assume all mission data from past 18 months was accessible to DJI infrastructure. Initiate security incident reporting if units were used for critical infrastructure or law enforcement missions.

gray_zone.status: Risk score 0.782 (CRITICAL) — Texas DTPA lawsuit filed Feb 18, 2026, no response in 45+ days, product line discontinued.
gray_zone.development: Texas filed 7 counts under DTPA in Collin County seeking injunctive relief and civil penalties up to $10K per violation. Anzu has filed no response as of April 4, leaving case frozen for 45+ days. Independent teardown confirms PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise, with matching board part numbers. Firmware is signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys, meaning DJI retains root key control. Anzu discontinued products citing adversary component shortage, confirming dependency.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Raptor series buyers face zero firmware sovereignty, no replacement parts pipeline, and potential civil litigation exposure if procurement violated state-level UAS security statutes. Estimated 200–400 units deployed across state/local agencies based on 2024 launch trajectory.
gray_zone.action: Issue stop-use order for all Anzu Raptor units. Document procurement decision chain and vendor representations. Consult legal on whether your jurisdiction has DTPA-equivalent statutes that could create liability. Plan fleet replacement by Q3 2026.

gray_zone.status: Risk score 0.537 (HIGH) — FCC Covered List as of Dec 22, 2025, teardown confirms DJI Air 3 hardware identity.
gray_zone.development: Specta Air teardown reveals identical PCBs to DJI Air 3, same DJI P1 chipset, with RF emission reports differing only by date and logo. No licensing relationship with DJI has been acknowledged by Cogito, suggesting unlicensed derivative or gray market component sourcing. FCC retroactive revocation authority applies, meaning existing authorizations can be pulled. No new models can receive FCC authorization going forward.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Specta Air buyers have counterfeit-risk hardware with zero supply chain transparency. If FCC revokes existing authorizations, units become illegal to operate in US airspace regardless of purchase date. Estimated 50–150 units in circulation based on limited market presence.
gray_zone.action: Treat Specta Air as non-compliant effective immediately. Do not issue new purchase orders. If units are in procurement pipeline, reject delivery and demand refund. For deployed units, initiate phase-out by Q2 2026.

gray_zone.status: Risk score 0.525 (HIGH) — Named in Section 1709(a)(1) FY2025 NDAA, FCC Covered List, partial authorization revocations in Jan 2026.
gray_zone.development: Autel is explicitly named in Section 1709(a)(1) of the FY2025 NDAA alongside DJI. FCC added Autel to Covered List on Dec 22, 2025, and revoked certain pre-ban authorizations in January 2026. Autel is seeking reconsideration at FCC, but no stay has been granted. Unlike Anzu/SkyRover/Cogito, Autel hardware is proprietary (not DJI derivative), but statutory prohibition remains regardless of technical independence. Dragonfish fixed-wing platform has notable deployments in public safety (Florida Sheriff, others).
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Autel EVO and Dragonfish operators face regulatory prohibition regardless of technical merit. FCC reconsideration process could take 6–18 months with uncertain outcome. Units may become uninsurable or face airspace access denial even if FCC authorizations remain technically valid. Estimated 800–1,500 units across federal, state, and local agencies.
gray_zone.action: Freeze new Autel purchases pending FCC reconsideration outcome. For existing deployments, establish contingency replacement timeline by Q4 2026. Monitor FCC docket for reconsideration decision (expected Q2/Q3 2026). If reconsideration fails, initiate immediate divestment.

supply_chain.signal: CRITICAL diversion risk — 40,000+ RPi 4B units confirmed in Geran-2/3 (Shahed-136/238) as 'Borscht Tracker V3'. RPi 5 confirmed in Molniya-2R reconnaissance drone. RPi-based tracker with mesh networking confirmed in Geran-5 jet drone (90kg warhead, 1000km range).
supply_chain.window: Immediate — diversion is active and documented by GUR. Export control enforcement could trigger allocation restrictions or distributor audits within 60–90 days.
supply_chain.action: Audit all Raspberry Pi components in your BOM. Document end-use and custody chain for compliance. Identify alternative compute modules (e.g., NXP i.MX 8M, TI AM62x) for future designs. Expect distributor questionnaires and potential allocation by Q3 2026.

supply_chain.signal: 82% probability of allocation-only access for defense by Q3 2026. 16 of 25 companion computer options (64% concentration) use Jetson platforms. 13 boards in Forge DB at risk. Concentration risk model confidence: 1.00.
supply_chain.window: Q3 2026 (5 months) — NVIDIA has history of tiering access during supply crunches. Defense programs will compete with automotive and industrial AI deployments.
supply_chain.action: Lock long-term supply agreements with distributors NOW for Orin NX and Orin Nano modules. Negotiate minimum quarterly allocations with price protection through Q4 2027. For programs in design phase, evaluate AMD Kria KR260 or Intel Movidius as second sources.

supply_chain.signal: 87% probability of allocation tightening in H2 2026. 15 of 52 thermal camera integrations (29% concentration) use Boson. AeroVironment Switchblade production ramp identified as demand driver. Cross-correlation model confidence: 0.87.
supply_chain.window: H2 2026 (6–9 months) — Teledyne FLIR has limited Boson fab capacity and Switchblade ramp will consume available output.
supply_chain.action: Submit 12-month forecast to Teledyne FLIR by April 30. Request allocation commitment in writing. For new programs, evaluate BAE MicroIR 640 or Lynred Pico640 Gen2 as qualified alternatives to avoid single-source dependency.

sources.name: DroneClear Forge DB Thermal Camera Analysis

watch_list.why: 17 of 29 mesh radio options (59% concentration) have compliance questions, but only 29 NDAA-compliant options exist — meaning there are zero hedge options if primary vendors stumble.
watch_list.trigger: If any of the 29 compliant vendors experiences supply disruption, gets acquired by non-compliant entity, or exits defense market, mesh comms becomes single-point failure for swarming and BVLOS ops.

watch_list.why: 5 companion computer boards depend on QRB5165, which is mature product (2020 launch). Qualcomm has not announced successor roadmap publicly, creating upgrade uncertainty for 2027+ programs.
watch_list.trigger: If Qualcomm announces QRB5165 EOL or allocates production to mobile/automotive ahead of defense, Blue UAS Framework certification timelines could slip 12–18 months while vendors re-spin hardware.

watch_list.why: 79% probability that 3 simultaneous FCC firmware deadlines ground uncertified gray zone drones in January 2027, affecting residual Anzu/SkyRover/Autel inventory still in service.
watch_list.trigger: If FCC publishes final certification requirements in Q3 2026 and no gray zone vendor can meet them, agencies will face hard cutoff with 4–6 month replacement window during winter procurement freeze.

predictions.event: FLIR Boson 640 tightens — 3 Boson units tracked, AeroV Switchblade ramp drives allocation
predictions.hedge: Execute sole-source justification for 18-month Boson buy by May 15. Simultaneously fund integration and test of BAE MicroIR 640 as drop-in replacement to compress qualification timeline to 120 days if Boson allocation fails.

predictions.event: Jetson Orin NX goes allocation-only for defense (13 boards in Forge DB at risk)
predictions.hedge: Negotiate Orin NX supply agreement with Arrow or Avnet that includes quarterly minimum allocation and 15% price ceiling through Q4 2027. Budget $75K–$150K for NRE to port software stack to AMD Kria KR260 as Plan B architecture.

predictions.event: FCC firmware cliff — 3 simultaneous deadlines ground uncertified gray zone drones
predictions.hedge: Complete fleet audit by June 2026. For any gray zone units still in service, earmark replacement budget in FY27 Q1 (Oct 2026) and pre-position POs for Blue UAS alternatives. Assume zero residual value for Anzu/SkyRover/non-compliant Autel inventory after Dec 31, 2026.

signal_summary.top_concern: 467 airframes grounded with gray zone enforcement accelerating and no firmware certification pathway
